finding: AudioPlaybackStats adds browser-visible playback-path quality evidence: underrun duration, underrun event count, total playback duration, and latency statistics. These values update no more than once per second and only while the context is running and either the document is fully active and visible or microphone permission is granted. Underruns indicate frames were not delivered on time, but neither zero underruns nor observed underruns prove human audibility.
design_implication: When AudioPlaybackStats exists, capture a bounded visible-session sample after the diagnostic pulse and classify unsupported, update-not-permitted, invalid, no-duration, no-underrun-observed, and underrun-observed states separately. Keep physical acoustic output and human audibility as independent evidence layers.

verification.environment: isolated Node v22.16.0 runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The interface is in an editor's draft and may change.
Browser support may be absent, especially on current mobile Safari.
Statistics update at most once per second and can be stale when update conditions are not met.
falsification_routes: Make a hidden document without microphone permission claim fresh playback statistics.
Accept impossible latency ordering or underrun duration greater than total duration.
Infer acoustic output or human audibility from zero underruns.
Treat unsupported playbackStats as evidence of silence.
privacy_boundary: Do not request microphone permission solely to unlock playback statistics. Retain only aggregate counters and coarse latency values needed for the bounded test session; do not retain device identifiers, microphone content, or cross-session fingerprints.
